articles.title: Ukraine busts ‘bot farm’ supplying thousands of fake Telegram accounts to Russian spies
articles.summary: Ukrainian authorities have dismantled a so-called “bot farm” that police say was supplying thousands of fake social media accounts to Russian intelligence services for use in disinformation campaigns against Ukraine.

articles.title: The Gentlemen ransomware now uses SystemBC for bot-powered attacks
articles.summary: A SystemBC proxy malware botnet of more than 1,570 hosts, believed to be corporate victims, has been discovered following an investigation into a Gentlemen ransomware attack carried out by a gang affiliate. [...]

articles.title: Seiko USA website defaced as hacker claims customer data theft
articles.summary: The Seiko USA website was defaced over the weekend, displaying a message from attackers claiming they stole its Shopify customer database and threatening to leak it unless a ransom is paid. [...]

articles.title: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) Enables RCE via Malicious GGUF Model Files
articles.summary: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in SGLang that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution on susceptible systems. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5760, carries a CVSS score of 9.8 out of 10.0. It has been described as a case of command injection lea

articles.title: Crypto infrastructure company blames $290 million theft on North Korean hackers
articles.summary: A theft of nearly $300 million worth of cryptocurrency has been attributed to hackers from North Korea, as the industry grapples with the fallout of a wide-ranging incident involving multiple prominent platforms.

articles.title: Serial-to-IP Converter Flaws Expose OT and Healthcare Systems to Hacking
articles.summary: Forescout researchers discovered 20 new vulnerabilities in Lantronix and Silex products and described theoretical attack scenarios. The post Serial-to-IP Converter Flaws Expose OT and Healthcare Systems to Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Microsoft: Teams increasingly abused in helpdesk impersonation attacks
articles.summary: Microsoft is warning of threat actors increasingly abusing external Microsoft Teams collaboration and relying on legitimate tools for access and lateral movement on enterprise networks. [...]

articles.title: Cyberattack at French identity document agency may have exposed personal data
articles.summary: A cyberattack targeting a French government website used to manage identity documents and driver’s licenses may have exposed users’ personal data, the Interior Ministry said.

articles.title: The backup myth that is putting businesses at risk
articles.summary: Backups protect data, but don't keep your business running during downtime. Datto shows why BCDR is essential to keep operations running during ransomware and outages. [...]

articles.title: Bluesky blames app outage on ‘sophisticated’ DDoS attack
articles.summary: The decentralized social network said the incident began on April 15, when the company received reports of intermittent outages affecting the app.

articles.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Vercel Hack, Push Fraud, QEMU Abused, New Android RATs Emerge & More
articles.summary: Monday’s recap shows the same pattern in different places. A third-party tool becomes a way in, then leads to internal access. articles.title: British Scattered Spider Hacker Pleads Guilty in the US
articles.summary: Tyler Buchanan admitted in court to hacking into various companies, defrauding them, and stealing cryptocurrency from multiple individuals. The post British Scattered Spider Hacker Pleads Guilty in the US appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: British hacker tied to Scattered Spider campaign pleads guilty in $8M scheme
articles.summary: A British hacker pleaded guilty in U.S. federal court to participating in a sweeping cybercrime campaign that siphoned at least $8 million in cryptocurrency from companies and individuals, federal prosecutors said.

articles.title: ​​Supply Chain Compromise Impacts Axios Node Package Manager​
articles.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is releasing this alert to provide guidance in response to the software supply chain compromise of the Axios node package manager (npm).1 Axios is an HTTP client for JavaScript that developers commonly use in Node.js and browser environment

articles.title: CISA Adds Eight Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog

articles.title: Hackers Abuse QEMU for Defense Evasion
articles.summary: The machine emulator has been abused in at least two different campaigns distributing ransomware and remote access tools. The post Hackers Abuse QEMU for Defense Evasion appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Bluesky Disrupted by Sophisticated DDoS Attack
articles.summary: A pro-Iran hacker group has taken credit for the attack on Bluesky, which appears to have lasted 24 hours. The post Bluesky Disrupted by Sophisticated DDoS Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Anthropic MCP Design Vulnerability Enables RCE, Threatening AI Supply Chain
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a critical "by design" weakness in the Model Context Protocol's (MCP) architecture that could pave the way for remote code execution and have a cascading effect on the artificial intelligence (AI) supply chain. articles.title: Half of the 6 Million Internet-Facing FTP Servers Lack Encryption
articles.summary: The continued use of the half-century-old protocol exposes enterprises and end users to various types of attacks. The post Half of the 6 Million Internet-Facing FTP Servers Lack Encryption appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Next.js Creator Vercel Hacked
articles.summary: Vercel confirmed suffering a breach after a hacker claiming to be part of ShinyHunters offered to sell stolen data for $2 million. The post Next.js Creator Vercel Hacked appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Hackers Fail to Exploit Flaw in Discontinued TP-Link Routers
articles.summary: In-the-wild exploitation has been ongoing for a year, but no successful payload execution has been observed. The post Hackers Fail to Exploit Flaw in Discontinued TP-Link Routers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Researchers Detect ZionSiphon Malware Targeting Israeli Water, Desalination OT Systems
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malware called ZionSiphon that appears to be specifically designed to target Israeli water treatment and desalination systems. The malware has been codenamed ZionSiphon by Darktrace, highlighting its ability to set up persistence, tamper with local config

articles.summary: Web infrastructure provider Vercel has disclosed a security breach that allows bad actors to gain unauthorized access to "certain" internal Vercel systems. articles.title: Vercel confirms breach as hackers claim to be selling stolen data
articles.summary: Cloud development platform Vercel has disclosed a security incident after threat actors claimed to have breached its systems and are attempting to sell stolen data. [...]

articles.title: Critical flaw in Protobuf library enables JavaScript code execution
articles.summary: Proof-of-concept exploit code has been published for a critical remote code execution flaw in protobuf.js, a widely used JavaScript implementation of Google's Protocol Buffers. [...]

articles.summary: NAKIVO Inc. announced the general availability of NAKIVO Backup & Replication v11.2, focused on fast, reliable, and proactive data protection. [...]

articles.title: Tycoon 2FA Loses Phishing Kit Crown Amid Surge in Attacks
articles.summary: Threat actors are reusing Tycoon 2FA tools across other phishing kits following the platform’s disruption. The post Tycoon 2FA Loses Phishing Kit Crown Amid Surge in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: In 2024, compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches. Not phishing. Not weak passwords. articles.title: $13.74M Hack Shuts Down Sanctioned Grinex Exchange After Intelligence Claims
articles.summary: Grinex, a Kyrgyzstan-incorporated cryptocurrency exchange sanctioned by the U.K. and the U.S. last year, said it's suspending operations after it blamed Western intelligence agencies for a $13.74 million hack. articles.summary: Threat actors are exploiting security flaws in TBK DVR and end‑of‑life (EoL) TP-Link Wi-Fi routers to deploy Mirai-botnet variants on compromised devices, according to findings from Fortinet FortiGuard Labs and Palo Alto Networks Unit 42. articles.title: Payouts King ransomware uses QEMU VMs to bypass endpoint security
articles.summary: The Payouts King ransomware is using the QEMU emulator as a reverse SSH backdoor to run hidden virtual machines on compromised systems and bypass endpoint security. [...]

articles.title: Ransomware attack continues to disrupt healthcare in London nearly two years later
articles.summary: More than 18 months after a ransomware attack disrupted care at hospitals in South East London, documents show at least one NHS trust is still working without fully restored systems and managing large backlogs of delayed test results.

articles.title: Four arrested in latest ‘PowerOFF’ DDoS-for-hire takedown
articles.summary: More than 20 countries participated in a coordinated takedown of platforms selling cheap access to distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks.

articles.title: Ukraine confirms suspected APT28 campaign targeting prosecutors, anti-corruption agencies
articles.summary: The intrusions exploited vulnerabilities in the open-source Roundcube webmail platform that allow attackers to execute malicious code when a victim simply opens an email in their inbox.

articles.title: Inside an Underground Guide: How Threat Actors Vet Stolen Credit Card Shops
articles.summary: In cybercrime markets, trust isn't assumed, it's verified. Flare reveals how underground guides teach actors to evaluate carding shops based on data quality, reputation, and survivability. [...]

articles.title: Three Microsoft Defender Zero-Days Actively Exploited; Two Still Unpatched
articles.summary: Huntress is warning that threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed security flaws in Microsoft Defender to gain elevated privileges in compromised systems. The activity involves the exploitation of three vulnerabilities that are codenamed BlueHammer (requires GitHub sign-in), RedSun

articles.title: In defeat for Trump, House extends electronic spying program for just 10 days
articles.summary: The House passed stopgap legislation to extend a warrantless government surveillance power for 10 days, following a failed lobbying campaign by the Trump administration.

articles.title: Webinar: From phishing to fallout — Why MSPs must rethink both security and recovery
articles.summary: Cyberattacks are evolving faster than many MSP and corporate defenses can keep up, with phishing driving much of today's cybercrime. Join our upcoming webinar to learn how to combine security and recovery strategies to reduce risk and maintain business continuity. [...]

articles.title: Another DraftKings Hacker Sentenced to Prison
articles.summary: Kamerin Stokes sold stolen credentials through an online marketplace even after pleading guilty to his role in the DraftKings attack. The post Another DraftKings Hacker Sentenced to Prison appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: The remote code execution vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-34197 came to light in early April. The post Recent Apache ActiveMQ Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA flags Apache ActiveMQ flaw as actively exploited in attacks
articles.summary: CISA warned that attackers are now exploiting a high-severity Apache ActiveMQ vulnerability, which was patched earlier this month after going undetected for 13 years. [...]

articles.title: Two North Korean IT Worker Scheme Facilitators Jailed in the US
articles.summary: Kejia Wang and Zhenxing Wang compromised the identities of dozens of US persons to help land jobs at over 100 companies. The post Two North Korean IT Worker Scheme Facilitators Jailed in the US appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: ZionSiphon Malware Targets ICS in Water Facilities
articles.summary: The malware is configured to operate on systems associated with Israeli water treatment and desalination plants. The post ZionSiphon Malware Targets ICS in Water Facilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Cursor AI Vulnerability Exposed Developer Devices
articles.summary: An indirect prompt injection could be chained with a sandbox bypass and Cursor’s remote tunnel feature for shell access to machines. The post Cursor AI Vulnerability Exposed Developer Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: NIST Limits CVE Enrichment After 263% Surge in Vulnerability Submissions
articles.summary: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) has announced changes to the way it handles cybersecurity vulnerabilities and exposures (CVEs) listed in its National Vulnerability Database (NVD), stating it will only enrich those that fulfil certain conditions owing to an explosion in CVE

articles.title: Man gets 30 months for selling thousands of hacked DraftKings accounts
articles.summary: 23-year-old Kamerin Stokes of Memphis, Tennessee, was sentenced to 30 months in prison for selling access to tens of thousands of hacked DraftKings accounts. [...]

articles.title: Recently leaked Windows zero-days now exploited in attacks
articles.summary: Threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed Windows security vulnerabilities in attacks aimed at gaining SYSTEM or elevated administrator permissions. [...]
